Identification.LongTitle: An Act respecting transparency and accountability in relation to certain commitments Canada has made under the Convention on Biological Diversity
Identification.BillSponsor: MINISTER OF ENVIRONMENT AND CLIMATE CHANGEâ

Provision.Text: Her Excellency the Governor General recommends to the House of Commons the appropriation of public revenue under the circumstances, in the manner and for the purposes set out in a measure entitled âAn Act respecting transparency and accountability in relation to certain commitments Canada has made under the Convention on Biological Diversityâ.

This enactment imposes certain duties on the Minister of the Environment to promote transparency and accountability in relation to certain commitments that Canada has made under the Convention on Biological Diversity.

Heading.TitleText: An Act respecting transparency and accountability in relation to certain commitments Canada has made under the Convention on Biological Diversity

TableOfProvisions.Label: â

Heading.TitleText: National Biodiversity Strategy and Action Plan

TableOfProvisions.Text: National biodiversity strategy and action plan

Provision.Text: Whereas the Government of Canada recognizes that nature is an integral part of Canadaâs identity and history;
Whereas the Government of Canada recognizes that biodiversity, including the diversity of species and ecosystems, has intrinsic value;

Whereas the Government of Canada recognizes that biodiversity must be valued, conserved, restored and wisely used, maintaining ecosystem services, sustaining a healthy planet and delivering benefits essential for all people;
Whereas there is clear scientific evidence that biodiversity is declining at an alarming rate, which poses a pressing and serious threat to nature, human well-being and economic prosperity;

Whereas, as a party to the Convention on Biological Diversity, Canada is committed to contributing to the attainment of global targets and long-term goals and to the fulfillment of the vision of a world living in harmony with nature by 2050;

Whereas the Government of Canada recognizes that the development and implementation of measures to address biodiversity loss should contribute to making Canadaâs economy more resilient, inclusive and competitive;

Whereas the Government of Canada recognizes that addressing biodiversity loss requires transformative, immediate and ambitious action by all governments in Canada, the Indigenous peoples of Canada, the private sector, non-governmental organizations and individual Canadians and welcomes opportunities for collaboration in taking such action;

Whereas the Government of Canada is committed to respecting the rights recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982 and the rights affirmed in the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples and to strengthening its collaborative partnerships with the Indigenous peoples of Canada in developing and implementing measures to address biodiversity loss, including by taking Indigenous knowledge into account when carrying out the purposes of this Act;

Whereas the Government of Canada recognizes that First Nations, Inuit and the MÃ©tis have distinctive relationships with lands, territories, waters and other resources and hold an important role as stewards of biodiversity;

And whereas the Government of Canada recognizes that the protection of the environment, including biodiversity, contributes to and promotes human well-being, including the ability of present and future generations to effectively enjoy all human rights;
Now, therefore, His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Nature Accountability Act.
The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: advisory committeeâmeans the committee established by the Minister under subsection 7(1).â(comitÃ© consultatif)
Conference of the Partiesâmeans the body established under Article 23 of the Convention.â(ConfÃ©rence des Parties)

Conventionâmeans the Convention on Biological Diversity, which entered into force on December 29, 1993, as amended from time to time, to the extent that the amendment is in force for Canada.â(Convention)
Convention Secretariatâmeans the secretariat established under Article 24 of the Convention.â(secrÃ©tariat de la Convention)

global targetsâmeans the global targets set by the Conference of the Parties from time to time, such as the 23 targets set out in the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework adopted by the Conference of the Parties on December 19, 2022.â(cibles mondiales)
Indigenous knowledgeâmeans the Indigenous knowledge of the Indigenous peoples of Canada.â(connaissances autochtones)
Indigenous peoples of Canadaâhas the meaning assigned by the definition aboriginal peoples of Canada in subsection 35(2) of the Constitution Act, 1982.â(peuples autochtones du Canada)

long-term goalsâmeans the four global long-term goals for 2050 set out in the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework adopted by the Conference of the Parties on December 19, 2022.â(objectifs Ã long terme)
Ministerâmeans the Minister of the Environment.â(ministre)

national biodiversity strategy and action planâmeans a national biodiversity strategy and action plan established under section 5.â(stratÃ©gies et plans dâaction nationaux pour la biodiversitÃ©)

2050 visionâmeans the shared vision of a world living in harmony with nature by 2050 that is referred to in the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework adopted by the Conference of the Parties on December 19, 2022.â(vision 2050)

Section.Text: This Act is binding on His Majesty in right of Canada.
The purpose of this Act is to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: recognize that Canada is committed, under the Convention, to contributing to the attainment of the global targets and long-term goals and to the fulfillment of the 2050 vision;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: promote the transparency and accountability of the Government of Canada in respect of the development and implementation of measures aimed at meeting those commitments; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: encourage collaboration within the federal public administration, as well as between the Government of Canada and the governments of the provinces, the Indigenous peoples of Canada and municipal governments, in the development and implementation of such measures.

Heading.TitleText: National Biodiversity Strategy and Action Plan

Section.MarginalNote: National biodiversity strategy and action plan

Subsection.Text: The Minister must establish a national biodiversity strategy and action plan and submit it to the Convention Secretariat on or before each submission deadline set under the Convention.

Each national biodiversity strategy and action plan must set out any measures taken or proposed to be taken by the Government of Canada that are intended to contribute to the attainment of the global targets and long-term goals, including any mechanisms for integrating biodiversity considerations into decision-making processes.

A national biodiversity strategy and action plan may set out any other information that the Minister considers appropriate, including information respecting measures intended to contribute to the attainment of the global targets and long-term goals that are taken or proposed to be taken by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the governments of the provinces, the Indigenous peoples of Canada and municipal governments; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Government of Canada in collaboration with the governments of the provinces, the Indigenous peoples of Canada, municipal governments or any other parties.

Subsection.Text: When establishing a national biodiversity strategy and action plan, the Minister must take into account

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the best scientific and technological knowledge relevant to the conservation and restoration of nature, including biodiversity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: Indigenous knowledge relevant to the conservation and restoration of nature, including biodiversity;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the rights affirmed in the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples, including the right to self-determination;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the precautionary principle, which provides that, if there is a threat of significant reduction or loss of biodiversity, a lack of full scientific certainty should not be used as a reason for postponing cost-effective measures to avoid or minimize such a threat;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the principle of non-regression, which requires that appropriate measures be taken, based on current scientific and technological knowledge and on Indigenous knowledge, to prevent any regression of Canadaâs contributions to the attainment of the global targets and to make continuous progress in Canadaâs contributions to the attainment of the long-term goals;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the principle of intergenerational equity, which provides that it is important to meet the needs of the present generation without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the goal of advancing equality, including in light of the intersection of sex and gender with other identity factors; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: any submissions received under subsection (7) from the governments of the provinces, the Indigenous peoples of Canada and the advisory committee.

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, when establishing a national biodiversity strategy and action plan, the Minister must ensure respect for the rights of the Indigenous peoples of Canada recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982, including the inherent right of self-government.
When establishing a national biodiversity strategy and action plan, the Minister must consult with any federal minister that the Minister considers appropriate.

When establishing a national biodiversity strategy and action plan, the Minister must, in the manner that the Minister considers appropriate, provide the governments of the provinces, the Indigenous peoples of Canada, the advisory committee and interested persons, including any expert the Minister considers appropriate to consult, with the opportunity to make submissions.

The Minister must cause each national biodiversity strategy and action plan to be tabled in each House of Parliament on or before the 15th day on which that House is sitting after the day on which that national biodiversity strategy and action plan is submitted to the Convention Secretariat.

The Minister must publish each national biodiversity strategy and action plan, in the manner the Minister considers appropriate, as soon as feasible after it is submitted to the Convention Secretariat.
The Minister must prepare a national report and submit it to the Convention Secretariat on or before each submission deadline set under the Convention.
Each national report must set out

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an assessment of Canadaâs progress on its contributions to the attainment of the global targets and long-term goals as a result of the measures set out in its most recent national biodiversity strategy and action plan;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any corrective measures that are taken or proposed to be taken to address any failure to make progress on its contributions to the attainment of those targets or goals; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any other information that the Minister considers appropriate.

Subsection.Text: When preparing a national report, the Minister must take into account the considerations referred to in paragraphs 5(4)(a) to (g) and any submissions received under subsection (6).

For greater certainty, when preparing a national report, the Minister must ensure respect for the rights of the Indigenous peoples of Canada recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982, including the inherent right of self-government.
When preparing a national report, the Minister must consult with any federal minister that the Minister considers appropriate.

When preparing a national report, the Minister must, in the manner the Minister considers appropriate, provide the governments of the provinces, the Indigenous peoples of Canada and the advisory committee with the opportunity to make submissions.

The Minister must cause each national report to be tabled in each House of Parliament on or before the 15th day on which that House is sitting after the day on which that national report is submitted to the Convention Secretariat.
The Minister must publish each national report, in the manner the Minister considers appropriate, as soon as feasible after it is submitted to the Convention Secretariat.

The Minister must establish an advisory committee responsible for providing the Minister with independent advice on matters related to the carrying out of the purposes of this Act, including advice respecting

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: measures that may be taken by the Government of Canada in order to meet the commitments referred to in paragraph 4(a); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any other matter referred to it by the Minister.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may establish the terms of reference of the advisory committee. Any terms of reference must be published in the manner that the Minister considers appropriate.
The Minister may appoint as a member of the advisory committee one or more persons.
When deciding whether to appoint a member, the Minister must consider the need for the advisory committee as a whole to have

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: expertise in and knowledge of scientific disciplines relevant to the conservation and restoration of nature, including biodiversity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: Indigenous knowledge relevant to the conservation and restoration of nature, including biodiversity; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: expertise in and knowledge of biodiversity policy at the international, national and subnational levels, including the likely effects and efficacy of potential measures aimed at addressing biodiversity loss.

Section.Text: For greater certainty, national biodiversity strategies and action plans, national reports prepared under subsection 6(1) and terms of reference established under subsection 7(2) are not statutory instruments for the purposes of the Statutory Instruments Act.

A comprehensive review of the provisions and operation of this Act is to be undertaken no later than December 31, 2030 â and every 10 years after that date â by a committee of the Senate, of the House of Commons or of both Houses of Parliament that may be designated or established by the Senate or the House of Commons, or by both Houses of Parliament, as the case may be, for that purpose.
